blockingIssues.description: `src/engine/edge_calculator.py:216` requires `EV > 0` in addition to the documented `edge >= 0.03` threshold, so downstream pipeline filtering can reject bets that meet the feature contract's recommendation rule.

`src/pipeline/daily.py:517` does not orchestrate the full same-day offense/pitching/defense/bullpen/weather feature stack; it reuses persisted rows plus fill defaults, so normal daily inference can run on stale/defaulted inputs instead of freshly computed features.

`src/features/offense.py:149` and `src/features/pitching.py:180-186` never make the helper's `is_first_year=True` rookie path reachable in production, so first-year players do not actually fall back to league-average priors end-to-end.

`src/model/data_builder.py:649-670` invokes production feature generation without `roster_turnover_by_team`, so the promised >40% roster-turnover prior-weight reduction never runs outside isolated helper tests.

`src/pipeline/daily.py:392` sends the picks webhook before post-send persistence/freezing/settlement side effects; if one of those later steps fails, `main()` can emit a second failure-alert webhook in the same run, violating the max-one-webhook-per-run requirement.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document the Windows-safe startup and validation workflow explicitly: use `.factory/services.yaml` `*_windows` commands or direct `.venv\Scripts\python.exe` invocations for init, pytest, typecheck, and lint instead of `bash .factory/init.sh` or POSIX glob commands.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review `t23-settlement-rules` found the worker had to abandon failed `bash .factory/init.sh` attempts before using manifest-driven PowerShell init steps, and review `t26-discord-webhook` found justified deviations from POSIX-style validator commands even though `.factory/services.yaml` and `.factory/library/environment.md` already document the working Windows path.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Describe the repo's current narrowed fast-inference path for `t24` so workers do not assume the live CLI recomputes the full offense/pitching/defense/bullpen/weather stack while the pybaseball/pandas limitation remains unresolved.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review `t24-daily-orchestrator` found the skill still describes a full ingest→feature→predict pipeline, while `.factory/library/workspace-state.md` documents that the committed pipeline currently reuses persisted feature rows and skips full live recomputation due to the current pybaseball/pandas compatibility problem.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Expand the shared Marcel guidance to include the per-metric regression weights (offense 30 games, pitching 15 starts, defense 50 games), the league-average fallback for first-year players, and the >40% roster-turnover prior-weight reduction.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review `t25-marcel-blend` found that mission guidance still shows only a simplified single-weight formula, which no longer matches the intended repo contract for rookie and turnover handling.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Add Marcel-specific implementation and verification guidance—or move Marcel work out of the engine-worker scope—so workers validate rookie fallback and roster-turnover behavior end-to-end instead of only helper-level unit tests.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review `t25-marcel-blend` found the helper itself is well factored, but the worker procedure and checklist remain centered on edge/Kelly/settlement behavior and did not prompt end-to-end verification of the promised Marcel-specific rookie and turnover behaviors.

rejectedObservations.observation: `t21-edge-calculator` noted that the feature defines a persistent SQLite table inside `src/engine/edge_calculator.py` instead of `src/db.py`.
rejectedObservations.reason: already-documented; current AGENTS/library guidance already identifies `src/db.py` as the central schema entrypoint, so no additional shared-state update is needed

rejectedObservations.observation: `t26-discord-webhook` noted that engine-worker still prescribes POSIX-style validator commands on Windows.
rejectedObservations.reason: deduplicated into the broader systemic Windows-safe worker-skill guidance recommendation
